---
title: "Special Topics Bash Code"
author: "Monique White"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

# Bash code

## Download data
```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# =============================================================================
# Master FASTQ download script
# Studies: SRP112607 (4 runs), SRP141379 (128 runs), E-MTAB-14664 (6 runs)
# Total: 138 runs — FTP paths are fetched live from ENA API at runtime
# Resumable: re-run to skip completed files and resume partial transfers
# Integrity-checked: gzip-tested after download, MD5 verified at the end
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

BASE_DIR="/data/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data"
LOG_DIR="/data/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/logs"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"

# =============================================================================
# Core helpers
# =============================================================================

_is_complete() {
  local f="$1"
  [ -s "$f" ] || return 1
  [ -f "$f.done" ] && [ ! "$f" -nt "$f.done" ] && return 0
  case "$f" in
  *.gz) gzip -t "$f" 2>/dev/null ;;
*) return 0 ;;
esac
}

_human() { numfmt --to=iec --suffix=B --format='%.1f' "$1" 2>/dev/null || echo "${1}B"; }

_done_count=0
_total_count=0

download_one() {
  local url="$1" out="$2" expected="${3:-0}"
  _done_count=$((_done_count + 1))
  local prefix="[$_done_count/$_total_count] $(basename $out)"
  local sz=0
  [ -f "$out" ] && sz=$(stat -c %s "$out" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  local sz_msg=""
  [ "$expected" -gt 0 ] && sz_msg=" ($(_human $sz) / $(_human $expected))"
  printf '%s: checking%s... ' "$prefix" "$sz_msg" >&2
  
  if [ "$expected" -gt 0 ] && [ "$sz" -gt "$expected" ]; then
  echo "oversized, redownloading" >&2
  rm -f "$out" "$out.done"
  elif _is_complete "$out"; then
  : > "$out.done"
  echo "valid, skip" >&2
  return 0
  elif [ "$sz" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "partial, resuming" >&2
  rm -f "$out.done"
  wget -c -q --show-progress --tries=10 --timeout=60 --retry-connrefused -O "$out" "$url" || true
  if _is_complete "$out"; then
  : > "$out.done"
  echo "$prefix: resumed OK" >&2
  return 0
  fi
  echo "$prefix: WARN integrity check failed after resume; redownloading from scratch" >&2
  rm -f "$out" "$out.done"
  else
    echo "missing, downloading" >&2
  rm -f "$out.done"
  fi
  
  mkdir -p "${out%/*}"
  wget -q --show-progress --tries=10 --timeout=60 --retry-connrefused -O "$out" "$url"
  _is_complete "$out" || {
    echo "$prefix: ERR corrupted after download (re-run script)" >&2
    rm -f "$out" "$out.done"
    return 1
  }
  : > "$out.done"
  echo "$prefix: done" >&2
}

# Fetch FTP paths + MD5 + sizes from ENA API for a given accession
# Prints: run_accession <TAB> ftp_url1;ftp_url2 <TAB> md5_1;md5_2 <TAB> bytes_1;bytes_2
fetch_ena_info() {
  local accession="$1"
  curl -sS --retry 5 --retry-delay 3 \
  "https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ena/portal/api/filereport?accession=${accession}&result=read_run&fields=run_accession,fastq_ftp,fastq_md5,fastq_bytes&format=tsv" \
  | tail -n +2   # strip header
}

# =============================================================================
# Download one study worth of runs
# Arguments: study_id  base_subdir  [list of run accessions...]
# =============================================================================
download_study() {
  local study="$1"
  local subdir="$2"
  shift 2
  local runs=("$@")
  
  echo ""
  echo "============================================================"
  echo "  Study: $study  (${#runs[@]} runs)"
  echo "  Output: $BASE_DIR/$subdir"
  echo "============================================================"
  
  # Collect all file info up front
  declare -a all_urls all_outs all_sizes all_md5s
  all_urls=(); all_outs=(); all_sizes=(); all_md5s=()
  
  echo "Querying ENA API for FTP paths..." >&2
  for run in "${runs[@]}"; do
  local info
  info=$(fetch_ena_info "$run")
  if [ -z "$info" ]; then
  echo "WARN: No ENA data found for $run — skipping" >&2
  continue
  fi
  
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r run_acc ftp_urls md5s bytes; do
  [ -z "$ftp_urls" ] && continue
  IFS=';' read -ra urls  <<< "$ftp_urls"
  IFS=';' read -ra mds   <<< "$md5s"
  IFS=';' read -ra szs   <<< "$bytes"
  for i in "${!urls[@]}"; do
  local url="ftp://${urls[$i]}"
  local fname; fname=$(basename "${urls[$i]}")
  local out="$BASE_DIR/$subdir/$run_acc/$fname"
  local sz="${szs[$i]:-0}"
  local md="${mds[$i]:-}"
  all_urls+=("$url")
  all_outs+=("$out")
  all_sizes+=("$sz")
  all_md5s+=("$md  $out")
  done
  done <<< "$info"
  done
  
  _total_count=${#all_urls[@]}
    _done_count=0
    
    # Resume estimate
    local present=0 local_bytes=0 total_bytes=0
    for i in "${!all_outs[@]}"; do
    local exp=${all_sizes[$i]:-0}
    total_bytes=$((total_bytes + exp))
    local f=${all_outs[$i]}
    if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    local sz; sz=$(stat -c %s "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    [ "$sz" -gt "$exp" ] && sz=0
    local_bytes=$((local_bytes + sz))
    [ "$sz" -ge "$exp" ] && [ "$exp" -gt 0 ] && present=$((present + 1))
    fi
    done
    local pct=$(( total_bytes > 0 ? local_bytes * 100 / total_bytes : 0 ))
    echo "Resume estimate: $present/$_total_count files complete ($(_human $local_bytes) / $(_human $total_bytes) ≈ ${pct}%)."
    
    # Download
    for i in "${!all_urls[@]}"; do
    download_one "${all_urls[$i]}" "${all_outs[$i]}" "${all_sizes[$i]:-0}"
    done
    
    # MD5 check
    echo ""
    echo "Verifying MD5 checksums for $study..."
    local md5_input=""
    for entry in "${all_md5s[@]}"; do
    [ -n "$entry" ] && md5_input+="$entry"$'\n'
    done
    if [ -n "$md5_input" ]; then
    echo "$md5_input" | md5sum -c --quiet && echo "All checksums OK for $study" || echo "WARN: Some checksums failed for $study — check above"
    else
      echo "No MD5s available to check for $study"
    fi
    
    echo "Done: $study → $BASE_DIR/$subdir"
  }
  
  # =============================================================================
  # Study definitions — run accessions from metadata CSV
  # =============================================================================
  
  # --- SRP112607 (4 runs, 4 experiments) ---
  SRP112607_RUNS=(
    SRR5833360
    SRR5833361
    SRR5833362
    SRR5833363
  )
  
  # --- SRP141379 (128 runs, 16 experiments) ---
  SRP141379_RUNS=(
    SRR7048256 SRR7048257 SRR7048258 SRR7048259
    SRR7048260 SRR7048261 SRR7048262 SRR7048263
    SRR7048264 SRR7048265 SRR7048266 SRR7048267
    SRR7048268 SRR7048269 SRR7048270 SRR7048271
    SRR7048272 SRR7048273 SRR7048274 SRR7048275
    SRR7048276 SRR7048277 SRR7048278 SRR7048279
    SRR7048280 SRR7048281 SRR7048282 SRR7048283
    SRR7048284 SRR7048285 SRR7048286 SRR7048287
    SRR7048288 SRR7048289 SRR7048290 SRR7048291
    SRR7048292 SRR7048293 SRR7048294 SRR7048295
    SRR7048296 SRR7048297 SRR7048298 SRR7048299
    SRR7048300 SRR7048301 SRR7048302 SRR7048303
    SRR7048304 SRR7048305 SRR7048306 SRR7048307
    SRR7048308 SRR7048309 SRR7048310 SRR7048311
    SRR7048312 SRR7048313 SRR7048314 SRR7048315
    SRR7048316 SRR7048317 SRR7048318 SRR7048319
    SRR7048320 SRR7048321 SRR7048322 SRR7048323
    SRR7048324 SRR7048325 SRR7048326 SRR7048327
    SRR7048328 SRR7048329 SRR7048330 SRR7048331
    SRR7048332 SRR7048333 SRR7048334 SRR7048335
    SRR7048336 SRR7048337 SRR7048338 SRR7048339
    SRR7048340 SRR7048341 SRR7048342 SRR7048343
    SRR7048344 SRR7048345 SRR7048346 SRR7048347
    SRR7048348 SRR7048349 SRR7048350 SRR7048351
    SRR7048352 SRR7048353 SRR7048354 SRR7048355
    SRR7048356 SRR7048357 SRR7048358 SRR7048359
    SRR7048360 SRR7048361 SRR7048362 SRR7048363
    SRR7048364 SRR7048365 SRR7048366 SRR7048367
    SRR7048368 SRR7048369 SRR7048370 SRR7048371
    SRR7048372 SRR7048373 SRR7048374 SRR7048375
    SRR7048376 SRR7048377 SRR7048378 SRR7048379
    SRR7048380 SRR7048381 SRR7048382 SRR7048383
  )
  
  # --- E-MTAB-14664 (6 runs) — already handled by download_ERP171667.sh ---
  # Included here for completeness / in case you want one unified script.
  # Comment this out if ERP171667 is already downloading separately.
  EMTAB14664_RUNS=(
    ERR14856082
    ERR14856083
    ERR14856084
    ERR14856085
    ERR14856086
    ERR14856087
  )
  
  # =============================================================================
  # Run all studies sequentially
  # =============================================================================
  
  echo "Starting downloads: $(date)"
  echo "Output root: $BASE_DIR"
  
  download_study "SRP112607"   "SRP112607"   "${SRP112607_RUNS[@]}"
  download_study "SRP141379"   "SRP141379"   "${SRP141379_RUNS[@]}"
  download_study "E-MTAB-14664" "E-MTAB-14664/ERP171667" "${EMTAB14664_RUNS[@]}"
  
  echo ""
  echo "============================================================"
  echo "  All studies complete: $(date)"
  echo "  Files saved under: $BASE_DIR"
  echo "============================================================"
```


FASTQC
```
#!/bin/bash

# ============================================================
# run_fastqc.sh
# Runs FastQC on all .fastq.gz files across three datasets,
# then aggregates results with MultiQC
# ============================================================

echo "Starting FastQC pipeline: $(date)"

# --- Create output directories ---
mkdir -p fastqc_output
mkdir -p multiqc_output

# --- Run FastQC ---
echo "Running FastQC..."
find raw_data/SRP112607/ raw_data/SRP141379/ raw_data/E-MTAB-14664/ \
    -name "*.fastq.gz" ! -name "*.done" \
    -type f | xargs fastqc --threads 8 -o fastqc_output/

echo "FastQC complete: $(date)"

# --- Run MultiQC ---
echo "Running MultiQC..."
multiqc fastqc_output/ -o multiqc_output/

echo "MultiQC complete: $(date)"
echo "Pipeline finished: $(date)"



Trimming data
#!/bin/bash

# ============================================================
# run_trimmomatic.sh
# Removes first 15 bases from paired-end .fastq.gz files
# across three datasets using Cutadapt
# ============================================================

echo "Starting Cutadapt pipeline: $(date)"

# --- Create output directory ---
#mkdir -p trimmed_data

# --- Loop through all _1.fastq.gz files ---
find /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/SRP112607/ /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/SRP141379/ /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/E-MTAB-14664/ \
    -name "*_1.fastq.gz" ! -name "*.done" \
    -type f | while read R1; do

    # Derive the R2 filename by replacing _1.fastq.gz with _2.fastq.gz
    R2="${R1/_1.fastq.gz/_2.fastq.gz}"

    # Get the sample name (e.g. SRR5833363) from the filename
    SAMPLE=$(basename "$R1" _1.fastq.gz)

    # Define output filenames
    R1_OUT="trimmed_data/${SAMPLE}_1_trimmed.fastq.gz"
    R2_OUT="trimmed_data/${SAMPLE}_2_trimmed.fastq.gz"
    R1_UNPAIRED="trimmed_data/${SAMPLE}_1_unpaired.fastq.gz"
    R2_UNPAIRED="trimmed_data/${SAMPLE}_2_unpaired.fastq.gz"

    echo "Trimming $SAMPLE: $(date)"

    cutadapt \
        -u 15 \
        -U 15 \
        -j 8 \
        -o "$R1_OUT" \
        -p "$R2_OUT" \
        "$R1" \
        "$R2"


    echo "Done $SAMPLE: $(date)"

done

echo "Cutadapt pipeline finished: $(date)"

#!/bin/bash

echo "Starting Cutadapt for E-MTAB-14664: $(date)"

find /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/E-MTAB-14664/ -name "*_1.fastq.gz" ! -name "*.done" -type f | while read R1; do

    R2="${R1/_1.fastq.gz/_2.fastq.gz}"
    SAMPLE=$(basename "$R1" _1.fastq.gz)
    R1_OUT="trimmed_data/${SAMPLE}_1_trimmed.fastq.gz"
    R2_OUT="trimmed_data/${SAMPLE}_2_trimmed.fastq.gz"

    echo "Trimming $SAMPLE: $(date)"
    cutadapt -u 15 -U 15 -j 8 -o "$R1_OUT" -p "$R2_OUT" "$R1" "$R2"
    echo "Done $SAMPLE: $(date)"

done

echo "Finished: $(date)"
Merging data ()
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Merge per-lane trimmed FASTQs into per-biological-replicate FASTQs
# Study: SRP141379 (Zhu et al. 2018) — CAL51, CC-671 treatment
# 16 biological replicates across 4 conditions x 2 timepoints
# Each biological replicate was sequenced across 8 lanes → merged here into 1

set -euo pipefail

TRIMMED="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/trimmed_data"
OUT="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/merged_data"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

echo "Starting lane merging — $(date)"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper function: merge R1 and R2 for a set of SRR runs into one output sample
# Usage: merge_sample <sample_name> <SRR1> <SRR2> ... 
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
merge_sample() {
  local name="$1"; shift
  local srrs=("$@")
  local r1_files=() r2_files=()

  for srr in "${srrs[@]}"; do
    r1_files+=("$TRIMMED/${srr}_1_trimmed.fastq.gz")
    r2_files+=("$TRIMMED/${srr}_2_trimmed.fastq.gz")
  done

  echo "  Merging ${#srrs[@]} lanes → ${name} ..."
  cat "${r1_files[@]}" > "$OUT/${name}_R1.fastq.gz"
  cat "${r2_files[@]}" > "$OUT/${name}_R2.fastq.gz"
  echo "  Done: ${name}"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DMSO controls — 6hr (2 biological replicates)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "[1/16] DMSO_6hr_rep1 (SRX3979816: SRR7048256–263)"
merge_sample "DMSO_6hr_rep1" \
  SRR7048256 SRR7048257 SRR7048258 SRR7048259 \
  SRR7048260 SRR7048261 SRR7048262 SRR7048263

echo "[2/16] DMSO_6hr_rep2 (SRX3979817: SRR7048264–271)"
merge_sample "DMSO_6hr_rep2" \
  SRR7048264 SRR7048265 SRR7048266 SRR7048267 \
  SRR7048268 SRR7048269 SRR7048270 SRR7048271

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DMSO controls — 24hr (2 biological replicates)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "[3/16] DMSO_24hr_rep1 (SRX3979818: SRR7048272–279)"
merge_sample "DMSO_24hr_rep1" \
  SRR7048272 SRR7048273 SRR7048274 SRR7048275 \
  SRR7048276 SRR7048277 SRR7048278 SRR7048279

echo "[4/16] DMSO_24hr_rep2 (SRX3979819: SRR7048280–287)"
merge_sample "DMSO_24hr_rep2" \
  SRR7048280 SRR7048281 SRR7048282 SRR7048283 \
  SRR7048284 SRR7048285 SRR7048286 SRR7048287

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CC-671 3uM — 6hr (3 biological replicates)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "[5/16] CC671_3uM_6hr_rep1 (SRX3979820: SRR7048288–295)"
merge_sample "CC671_3uM_6hr_rep1" \
  SRR7048288 SRR7048289 SRR7048290 SRR7048291 \
  SRR7048292 SRR7048293 SRR7048294 SRR7048295

echo "[6/16] CC671_3uM_6hr_rep2 (SRX3979821: SRR7048296–303)"
merge_sample "CC671_3uM_6hr_rep2" \
  SRR7048296 SRR7048297 SRR7048298 SRR7048299 \
  SRR7048300 SRR7048301 SRR7048302 SRR7048303

echo "[7/16] CC671_3uM_6hr_rep3 (SRX3979822: SRR7048304–311)"
merge_sample "CC671_3uM_6hr_rep3" \
  SRR7048304 SRR7048305 SRR7048306 SRR7048307 \
  SRR7048308 SRR7048309 SRR7048310 SRR7048311

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CC-671 3uM — 24hr (3 biological replicates)
# SRX3979823: SRR7048312–319  (3uM_24hr_rep1)
# SRX3979824: SRR7048320–327  (3uM_24hr_rep2)
# SRX3979825: SRR7048328–335  (3uM_24hr_rep3)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "[8/16] CC671_3uM_24hr_rep1 (SRX3979823: SRR7048312–319)"
merge_sample "CC671_3uM_24hr_rep1" \
  SRR7048312 SRR7048313 SRR7048314 SRR7048315 \
  SRR7048316 SRR7048317 SRR7048318 SRR7048319

echo "[9/16] CC671_3uM_24hr_rep2 (SRX3979824: SRR7048320–327)"
merge_sample "CC671_3uM_24hr_rep2" \
  SRR7048320 SRR7048321 SRR7048322 SRR7048323 \
  SRR7048324 SRR7048325 SRR7048326 SRR7048327

echo "[10/16] CC671_3uM_24hr_rep3 (SRX3979825: SRR7048328–335)"
merge_sample "CC671_3uM_24hr_rep3" \
  SRR7048328 SRR7048329 SRR7048330 SRR7048331 \
  SRR7048332 SRR7048333 SRR7048334 SRR7048335

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CC-671 10uM — 6hr (3 biological replicates)
# SRX3979826: SRR7048336–343  (10uM_6hr_rep1)
# SRX3979827: SRR7048344–351  (10uM_6hr_rep2)
# SRX3979828: SRR7048352–359  (10uM_6hr_rep3)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "[11/16] CC671_10uM_6hr_rep1 (SRX3979826: SRR7048336–343)"
merge_sample "CC671_10uM_6hr_rep1" \
  SRR7048336 SRR7048337 SRR7048338 SRR7048339 \
  SRR7048340 SRR7048341 SRR7048342 SRR7048343

echo "[12/16] CC671_10uM_6hr_rep2 (SRX3979827: SRR7048344–351)"
merge_sample "CC671_10uM_6hr_rep2" \
  SRR7048344 SRR7048345 SRR7048346 SRR7048347 \
  SRR7048348 SRR7048349 SRR7048350 SRR7048351

echo "[13/16] CC671_10uM_6hr_rep3 (SRX3979828: SRR7048352–359)"
merge_sample "CC671_10uM_6hr_rep3" \
  SRR7048352 SRR7048353 SRR7048354 SRR7048355 \
  SRR7048356 SRR7048357 SRR7048358 SRR7048359

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CC-671 10uM — 24hr (3 biological replicates)
# SRX3979829: SRR7048360–367  (10uM_24hr_rep1)
# SRX3979830: SRR7048368–375  (10uM_24hr_rep2)
# SRX3979831: SRR7048376–383  (10uM_24hr_rep3)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
echo "[14/16] CC671_10uM_24hr_rep1 (SRX3979829: SRR7048360–367)"
merge_sample "CC671_10uM_24hr_rep1" \
  SRR7048360 SRR7048361 SRR7048362 SRR7048363 \
  SRR7048364 SRR7048365 SRR7048366 SRR7048367

echo "[15/16] CC671_10uM_24hr_rep2 (SRX3979830: SRR7048368–375)"
merge_sample "CC671_10uM_24hr_rep2" \
  SRR7048368 SRR7048369 SRR7048370 SRR7048371 \
  SRR7048372 SRR7048373 SRR7048374 SRR7048375

echo "[16/16] CC671_10uM_24hr_rep3 (SRX3979831: SRR7048376–383)"
merge_sample "CC671_10uM_24hr_rep3" \
  SRR7048376 SRR7048377 SRR7048378 SRR7048379 \
  SRR7048380 SRR7048381 SRR7048382 SRR7048383

echo ""
echo "All done — $(date)"
echo "Merged files written to: $OUT"
echo ""
echo "Summary of output files:"
ls -lh "$OUT"/*.fastq.gz | awk '{print $5, $9}'
```

Running IRFinder
Build reference genome
```
singularity run IRFinder BuildRef -r IRFinder-repo/REF/Human-GRCh38-release100-100bp -M IRFinder-repo/REF/Mapabilities/hg38/MapabilityExclusion.100bp.bed.gz -e IRFinder-repo/REF/extra-input-files/RNA.SpikeIn.ERCC.fasta.gz -b IRFinder-repo/REF/extra-input-files/Human_hg38_nonPolyA_ROI.bed ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-100/gtf/homo_sapiens/Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.100.gtf.gz


singularity run IRFinder BuildRef -r IRFinder-repo/REF/Human-GRCh38-release100-150bp -M IRFinder-repo/REF/Mapabilities/hg38/MapabilityExclusion.100bp.bed.gz -e IRFinder-repo/REF/extra-input-files/RNA.SpikeIn.ERCC.fasta.gz -b IRFinder-repo/REF/extra-input-files/Human_hg38_nonPolyA_ROI.bed ftp://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-100/gtf/homo_sapiens/Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.100.gtf.gz
```

Zhu et al
```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run IRFinder on merged per-biological-replicate FASTQs
# Study: SRP141379 (Zhu et al. 2018) — CAL51, CC-671 treatment
# 16 biological replicates across 4 conditions x 2 timepoints

set -euo pipefail

MERGED="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/merged_data"
OUT="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379"
REF="IRFinder-repo/REF/Human-GRCh38-release100"
BIN="IRFinder-repo/bin/IRFinder"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

run_irfinder() {
  local srs="$1" r1="$2" r2="$3"
  echo "[$(date)] Running IRFinder: $srs"
  $BIN FastQ -r "$REF" -d "$OUT/${srs}_irfinder" \
    "$MERGED/$r1" "$MERGED/$r2"
  echo "[$(date)] Done: $srs"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6hr — DMSO (0uM)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_irfinder SRS3204967 DMSO_6hr_rep1_R1.fastq.gz DMSO_6hr_rep1_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204969 DMSO_6hr_rep2_R1.fastq.gz DMSO_6hr_rep2_R2.fastq.gz

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6hr — CC-671 3uM
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_irfinder SRS3204971 CC671_3uM_6hr_rep1_R1.fastq.gz CC671_3uM_6hr_rep1_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204972 CC671_3uM_6hr_rep2_R1.fastq.gz CC671_3uM_6hr_rep2_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204973 CC671_3uM_6hr_rep3_R1.fastq.gz CC671_3uM_6hr_rep3_R2.fastq.gz

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 6hr — CC-671 10uM
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_irfinder SRS3204977 CC671_10uM_6hr_rep1_R1.fastq.gz CC671_10uM_6hr_rep1_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204978 CC671_10uM_6hr_rep2_R1.fastq.gz CC671_10uM_6hr_rep2_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204979 CC671_10uM_6hr_rep3_R1.fastq.gz CC671_10uM_6hr_rep3_R2.fastq.gz

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 24hr — DMSO (0uM)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_irfinder SRS3204968 DMSO_24hr_rep1_R1.fastq.gz DMSO_24hr_rep1_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204970 DMSO_24hr_rep2_R1.fastq.gz DMSO_24hr_rep2_R2.fastq.gz

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 24hr — CC-671 3uM
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_irfinder SRS3204974 CC671_3uM_24hr_rep1_R1.fastq.gz CC671_3uM_24hr_rep1_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204975 CC671_3uM_24hr_rep2_R1.fastq.gz CC671_3uM_24hr_rep2_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204976 CC671_3uM_24hr_rep3_R1.fastq.gz CC671_3uM_24hr_rep3_R2.fastq.gz

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 24hr — CC-671 10uM
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
run_irfinder SRS3204980 CC671_10uM_24hr_rep1_R1.fastq.gz CC671_10uM_24hr_rep1_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204981 CC671_10uM_24hr_rep2_R1.fastq.gz CC671_10uM_24hr_rep2_R2.fastq.gz
run_irfinder SRS3204982 CC671_10uM_24hr_rep3_R1.fastq.gz CC671_10uM_24hr_rep3_R2.fastq.gz

echo ""
echo "All IRFinder jobs complete — $(date)"
```

Velde
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

TRIMMED="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/trimmed_data"
OUT="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667"
REF="IRFinder-repo/REF_150/Human-GRCh38-release100"
BIN="IRFinder-repo/bin/IRFinder"

mkdir -p "$OUT"

for num in $(seq 82 87); do
  sample="ERR148560${num}"
  outdir="$OUT/${sample}_irfinder"

  # Skip if already completed
  if [ -f "${outdir}/IRFinder-IR-dir.txt" ]; then
    echo "[$(date)] Skipping $sample — already complete"
    continue
  fi

  echo "[$(date)] Running IRFinder: $sample"
  $BIN FastQ -r "$REF" -d "$outdir" \
    "$TRIMMED/${sample}_1_trimmed.fastq.gz" \
    "$TRIMMED/${sample}_2_trimmed.fastq.gz"
  echo "[$(date)] Done: $sample"
done

echo ""
echo "All ERR IRFinder jobs complete — $(date)"


Iwai
# --- SRP112607 (Iwai et al. 2018) ---
echo "Processing SRP112607: $(date)"

for num in $(seq 360 363)
do
    echo "Processing SRR5833${num}: $(date)"
    IRFinder-repo/bin/IRFinder FastQ -r IRFinder-repo/REF/Human-GRCh38-release100 -d /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP112607/SRR5833${num}_irfinder /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/trimmed_data/SRR5833${num}_1_trimmed.fastq.gz /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/trimmed_data/SRR5833${num}_2_trimmed.fastq.gz
    echo "Done SRR5833${num}: $(date)"
done

echo "SRP112607 complete: $(date)"
echo "Pipeline finished: $(date)"
Filtering IRFinder output
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
# ==============================================================================
# filter_irfinder.R
#
# Finds every "IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt" file under a base directory, filters
# each one independently using the four IR-event selection criteria below,
# and writes one filtered output per sample.
#
# Criteria applied to each intron row (all must pass):
#   1. 0.7 <= SpliceLeft / SpliceRight <= 1.3
#   2. (SpliceLeft + SpliceRight) > 10
#   3. Coverage > 0.5
#   4. IRratio > 0.05
#
# Output naming:
#   For an input file at .../IR_quantification/<study>/<SAMPLE>_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt
#   the output is written as: filtered_ir_nondir_<SAMPLE>.txt
#   saved in the SAME directory as the source file (i.e. next to it, inside
#   the <SAMPLE>_irfinder folder).
#
# Usage (from an R terminal):
#   source("filter_irfinder.R")
#   run_filter("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification")
#
# Or from bash:
#   Rscript filter_irfinder.R /bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification
# ==============================================================================

IRFINDER_COLNAMES <- c(
  "Chr", "Start", "End", "Gene", "Null", "Strand", "ExcludedBases",
  "Coverage", "IntronDepth", "Intron25", "Intron50", "Intron75",
  "ExonToIntronReadsLeft", "ExonToIntronReadsRight",
  "IntronDepthFirst50bp", "IntronDepthLast50bp",
  "SpliceLeft", "SpliceRight", "SpliceExact", "IRratio", "Warnings"
)

# -- Core filter applied to a single sample's data.frame -----------------------
filter_ir_table <- function(df,
                             sl_sr_low = 0.7,
                             sl_sr_high = 1.3,
                             slsr_sum_min = 10,
                             coverage_min = 0.5,
                             ir_min = 0.05) {

  sl <- df$SpliceLeft
  sr <- df$SpliceRight

  # SL/SR ratio: guard against division by zero.
  # If SR == 0 and SL == 0 -> no info to reject on, treat as passing.
  # If SR == 0 and SL > 0  -> ratio undefined/infinite, fails.
  ratio <- ifelse(sr > 0, sl / sr, ifelse(sl == 0, 1, Inf))
  pass_ratio <- ratio >= sl_sr_low & ratio <= sl_sr_high

  pass_sum <- (sl + sr) > slsr_sum_min
  pass_cov <- df$Coverage > coverage_min
  pass_ir  <- !is.na(df$IRratio) & df$IRratio > ir_min

  keep <- pass_ratio & pass_sum & pass_cov & pass_ir
  list(
    filtered = df[keep, , drop = FALSE],
    n_total = nrow(df),
    n_pass_ratio = sum(pass_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_pass_sum = sum(pass_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_pass_cov = sum(pass_cov, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_pass_ir = sum(pass_ir, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_kept = sum(keep, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# -- Read one IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt file --------------------------------------
read_irfinder_file <- function(filepath) {
  df <- read.delim(filepath, header = FALSE, sep = "\t",
                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE, quote = "")
  n <- ncol(df)
  if (n < 20) {
    stop(sprintf("File %s has only %d columns, expected >= 20", filepath, n))
  }
  colnames(df)[1:21] <- IRFINDER_COLNAMES[1:min(21, n)]

  # Ensure numeric types where needed
  numeric_cols <- c("Coverage", "IntronDepth", "SpliceLeft", "SpliceRight",
                     "SpliceExact", "IRratio")
  for (col in numeric_cols) {
    df[[col]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[col]]))
  }
  df
}

# -- Extract sample name from the parent directory name ------------------------
# e.g. ".../SRS3204967_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt" -> "SRS3204967"
sample_name_from_path <- function(filepath) {
  parent_dir <- basename(dirname(filepath))
  sub("_irfinder$", "", parent_dir)
}

# -- Main driver: find & filter all files under a base directory ---------------
run_filter <- function(base_dir,
                        pattern = "^IRFinder-IR-nondir\\.txt$",
                        sl_sr_low = 0.7,
                        sl_sr_high = 1.3,
                        slsr_sum_min = 10,
                        coverage_min = 0.5,
                        ir_min = 0.05,
                        output_dir = NULL) {

  if (!dir.exists(base_dir)) {
    stop(sprintf("Base directory not found: %s", base_dir))
  }

  files <- list.files(base_dir, pattern = pattern, recursive = TRUE,
                       full.names = TRUE)

  if (length(files) == 0) {
    stop(sprintf("No files matching '%s' found under %s", pattern, base_dir))
  }

  cat(sprintf("Found %d IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt files under %s\n\n",
              length(files), base_dir))

  summary_rows <- list()

  for (fp in files) {
    sample <- sample_name_from_path(fp)
    cat(sprintf("Processing %s ... ", sample))

    df <- tryCatch(read_irfinder_file(fp), error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("SKIPPED (read error: %s)\n", conditionMessage(e)))
      NULL
    })
    if (is.null(df)) next

    result <- filter_ir_table(df, sl_sr_low, sl_sr_high, slsr_sum_min,
                               coverage_min, ir_min)

    out_dir <- if (is.null(output_dir)) dirname(fp) else output_dir
    if (!is.null(output_dir) && !dir.exists(output_dir)) {
      dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }
    out_path <- file.path(out_dir, sprintf("filtered_ir_nondir_%s.txt", sample))

    write.table(result$filtered, out_path, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE,
                row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE)

    cat(sprintf("%d / %d introns kept -> %s\n",
                result$n_kept, result$n_total, out_path))

    summary_rows[[sample]] <- data.frame(
      sample = sample,
      n_total = result$n_total,
      pass_slsr_ratio = result$n_pass_ratio,
      pass_slsr_sum = result$n_pass_sum,
      pass_coverage = result$n_pass_cov,
      pass_ir = result$n_pass_ir,
      n_kept = result$n_kept
    )
  }

  summary_df <- do.call(rbind, summary_rows)
  cat("\n--- Summary across all samples ---\n")
  print(summary_df, row.names = FALSE)

  invisible(summary_df)
}

# -- Allow running directly from bash: Rscript filter_irfinder.R <base_dir> ----
if (sys.nframe() == 0 && !interactive()) {
  args <- commandArgs(trailingOnly = TRUE)
  if (length(args) < 1) {
    stop("Usage: Rscript filter_irfinder.R <base_dir> [output_dir]")
  }
  base_dir <- args[1]
  output_dir <- if (length(args) >= 2) args[2] else NULL
  run_filter(base_dir, output_dir = output_dir)
}


Note
The filtered files only determine which rows (introns) are kept before running DESeq2.
In other words:
Raw IRFinder files
        ↓
Load counts into DESeq2 object
        ↓
Filtered IRFinder files
        ↓
Create whitelist of high-quality introns
        ↓
Apply IR ratio filter
        ↓
Subset DESeq2 object
        ↓
Run DESeq()
So the statistical analysis is performed on raw counts, but only for introns that passed the quality filtering defined by the filtered files. This is generally the preferred approach because DESeq2 expects integer count data, while the filtered files serve as a quality-control filter rather than as the source of counts.
does that mean the p-values are calculated fo rthe subset only, or for all?
The p-values are calculated only for the subset that remains after filtering.
In your script, the critical lines are:
dds_all <- dds_all_raw[
    rownames(dds_all_raw) %in% high_ir_introns_all,
]
dds_all <- DESeq(dds_all)
At this point:
dds_all_raw contains all introns loaded from the raw IRFinder files.
high_ir_introns_all contains only introns that passed:
the quality whitelist (from the filtered files), and
the IR ratio filter (>0.1 in at least 2 samples).
Then you subset:
dds_all <- dds_all_raw[ ... ]
After that, dds_all no longer contains the discarded introns.
When you run:
dds_all <- DESeq(dds_all)
DESeq2 only fits models for the rows present in dds_all.
Similarly,
res_6hr_3uM <- results(dds_all, ...)
returns p-values and adjusted p-values only for those retained introns.
What this means statistically
Suppose you started with:
180,000 introns in the raw files.
25,000 pass the quality filter.
8,000 pass the IR > 0.1 filter.
Then DESeq2 performs hypothesis tests for 8,000 introns, not 180,000.
The Benjamini–Hochberg multiple testing correction (padj) is also performed across those 8,000 tests, not across all original introns.
So this filtering strategy reduces the number of hypothesis tests, which generally increases statistical power because you're removing introns that were deemed untestable or essentially unexpressed before running the statistical tests. This kind of independent filtering is a common and appropriate approach, provided the filters are based on signal or quality measures and not on the treatment effect being tested. In your script, the filters are based on quality and baseline IR, not on differences between treatment groups, so they are independent of the differential test itself.

DESeq IR
Velde et al. 2026
setwd("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/")
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install(c("SummarizedExperiment", "DESeq2"), ask = FALSE)
library(DESeq2)

# ── Custom IRFinder DESeq2 function (Kept for Raw File Parsing) ─────────────
DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder = function(filePaths, designMatrix, designFormula){
  res=c()
  libsz=c()
  spl=c()
  irtest=read.table(filePaths[1])
  if (irtest[1,1]=="Chr"){irtest=irtest[-1,]}
  irnames=unname(apply(as.matrix(irtest),1,FUN=function(x){return(paste0(x[4],"/",x[1],":",x[2],"-",x[3],":",x[6]))}))
  n=1
  for (i in filePaths){
    print(paste0("processing file ",n," at ",i))
    irtab=read.table(i)
    if (irtab[1,1]=="Chr"){irtab=irtab[-1,]}
    tmp1=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,9]))
    tmp2=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,19]))
    tmp3=tmp1+tmp2
    tmp4=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,17]))
    tmp5=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,18]))
    tmp6=pmax(tmp4,tmp5, na.rm=T)
    res=cbind(res,tmp1)
    libsz=cbind(libsz,tmp2)
    spl=cbind(spl,tmp6)
    n=n+1
  }
  res.rd=round(res)
  libsz.rd=round(libsz)
  spl.rd=round(spl)
  colnames(res.rd)=paste("intronDepth",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
  rownames(res.rd)=irnames
  colnames(libsz.rd)=paste("totalSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
  rownames(libsz.rd)=irnames
  colnames(spl.rd)=paste("maxSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
  rownames(spl.rd)=irnames
  
  ir=c(rep("IR",dim(designMatrix)[1]),rep("Splice",dim(designMatrix)[1]))
  group=rbind(designMatrix,designMatrix)
  group$IRFinder=ir
  group$IRFinder=factor(group$IRFinder,levels=c("Splice","IR"))
  
  counts.IRFinder=cbind(res.rd,spl.rd)
  
  dd = DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts.IRFinder, colData = group, design = designFormula)
  sizeFactors(dd)=rep(1,dim(group)[1])
  rownames(dd)=irnames
  final=list(dd,res,libsz,spl)
  names(final)=c("DESeq2Object","IntronDepth","SpliceDepth","MaxSplice")
  return(final)
}

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Automatically maps raw paths to their corresponding filtered text file counterpart
filtered_path_for <- function(raw_path) {
  dir <- dirname(raw_path)
  sample <- sub("_irfinder$", "", basename(dir))
  file.path(dir, paste0("filtered_ir_nondir_", sample, ".txt"))
}

get_testable_introns <- function(raw_paths, min_frac = 0.5) {
  filtered_paths <- vapply(raw_paths, filtered_path_for, character(1))
  missing <- filtered_paths[!file.exists(filtered_paths)]
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop("Missing filtered file(s):\n  ", paste(missing, collapse = "\n  "),
         "\nRun filter_irfinder.R first for these samples.")
  }
  id_lists <- lapply(filtered_paths, function(fp) {
    df <- read.delim(fp, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    paste0(df$Gene, "/", df$Chr, ":", df$Start, "-", df$End, ":", df$Strand)
  })
  all_ids <- unlist(id_lists)
  tab <- table(all_ids)
  n <- length(filtered_paths)
  names(tab)[(tab / n) >= min_frac]
}

dir.create("results/ERP171667", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ==============================================================================
# ERP171667 DATA ANALYSIS
# ==============================================================================
# CORRECTED: Point these directly to the raw files so matrix building functions properly
paths <- c(
  file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856082_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"),
  file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856083_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"),
  file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856084_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"),
  file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856085_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"),
  file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856086_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"),
  file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856087_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt")
)

experiment <- data.frame(
  condition = factor(
    c("DMSO","DMSO","DMSO","T025","T025","T025"),
    levels = c("DMSO","T025")
  )
)

# Initialize with interaction formula layout
metaList <- DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder( 
  filePaths     = paths,
  designMatrix  = experiment,
  designFormula = ~ condition + IRFinder + condition:IRFinder
)

# ==============================================================================
# TWO-STAGE FILTERING STEP
# ==============================================================================
# Stage 1: Get high quality whitelisted introns from filtered files (min_frac check)
testable_introns <- get_testable_introns(paths, min_frac = 0.5)

# Map valid row names back to matrices to prevent bounds errors
intron_raw_mat <- metaList$IntronDepth
rownames(intron_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object)
intron_filtered_mat <- intron_raw_mat[testable_introns, ]

splice_raw_mat <- metaList$SpliceDepth
rownames(splice_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object)
splice_filtered_mat <- splice_raw_mat[testable_introns, ]

# Stage 2: Calculate actual IR ratios and filter for active signal (IR > 0.1)
all_ir_ratios <- intron_filtered_mat / (intron_filtered_mat + splice_filtered_mat + 0.01)

# Retain introns where at least 3 samples (50% of cohort) show an active IR ratio > 0.1
high_ir_introns <- rownames(all_ir_ratios)[rowSums(all_ir_ratios > 0.1) >= 3]

message("ERP171667: ", length(testable_introns), " introns pass quality screening.")
message("Value Filtering: ", length(high_ir_introns), " / ", length(testable_introns), 
        " introns also pass baseline expression threshold (IR ratio > 0.1 in >=3 samples).")

# Final clean subset of the dds object
dds <- metaList$DESeq2Object[rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object) %in% high_ir_introns, ]
dds <- DESeq(dds)

print("Available coefficients for ERP171667:")
print(resultsNames(dds))

# Extract the interaction term
res <- results(dds, name = "conditionT025.IRFinderIR")

write.csv(as.data.frame(res),
          file = "results/ERP171667/T025_vs_DMSO_1uM_24h.csv")

message("T-025 1uM 24h vs DMSO (Differential IR): ",
        sum(res$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
        " significant introns")

# ==============================================================================
# GENERATE CUSTOM INTRON RETENTION PCA
# ==============================================================================
message("Generating Intron Retention PCA plot...")

# Subset down to final filtered intron list for mapping
intron_counts <- intron_raw_mat[high_ir_introns, ]
splice_counts <- splice_raw_mat[high_ir_introns, ]
ir_ratio <- intron_counts / (intron_counts + splice_counts + 0.01)

log_ir <- log2(ir_ratio + 0.01)
log_ir_filtered <- log_ir[apply(log_ir, 1, var) > 0, ]

pca_output <- prcomp(t(log_ir_filtered), scale. = TRUE)
percentVar <- round(100 * (pca_output$sdev^2 / sum(pca_output$sdev^2)))

pca_data <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca_output$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_output$x[, 2],
  Condition = experiment$condition,
  Sample = paste0("Sample_", 1:nrow(experiment))
)

png("results/ERP171667/IR_Ratio_PCA.png", width = 800, height = 600, res = 120)
plot(pca_data$PC1, pca_data$PC2, 
     col = as.numeric(pca_data$Condition) + 1, 
     pch = 19, cex = 2,
     xlab = paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance"),
     ylab = paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance"),
     main = "PCA of Intron Retention Ratios (ERP171667)")
grid(lty = "dotted", col = "gray")
text(pca_data$PC1, pca_data$PC2, labels = pca_data$Sample, pos = 3, cex = 0.8)
legend("topright", legend = levels(pca_data$Condition), col = 2:3, pch = 19)
dev.off()

message("Done! PCA saved to results/ERP171667/IR_Ratio_PCA.png")



# setwd("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/")
# BiocManager::install(c("SummarizedExperiment", "DESeq2"), ask = FALSE)

#     tmp4=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,17]))
#     tmp5=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,18]))
