#   dd = DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts.IRFinder, colData = group, design = designFormula)
#   sizeFactors(dd)=rep(1,dim(group)[1])
#   rownames(dd)=irnames
#   final=list(dd,res,libsz,spl)
#   names(final)=c("DESeq2Object","IntronDepth","SpliceDepth","MaxSplice")
#   return(final)
# }
# 
# # ── Helper: map raw ERR path to its filtered counterpart ──────────────────────
# # e.g. .../ERR14856082_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt
# #   -> .../ERR14856082_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856082.txt
# filtered_path_for <- function(raw_path) {
#   dir <- dirname(raw_path)
#   sample <- sub("_irfinder$", "", basename(dir))
#   file.path(dir, paste0("filtered_ir_nondir_", sample, ".txt"))
# }
# 
# # ── Helper: determine testable introns for the ERP171667 cohort ───────────────
# get_testable_introns <- function(raw_paths, min_frac = 0.5) {
#   filtered_paths <- vapply(raw_paths, filtered_path_for, character(1))
#   missing <- filtered_paths[!file.exists(filtered_paths)]
#   if (length(missing) > 0) {
#     stop("Missing filtered file(s):\n  ", paste(missing, collapse = "\n  "),
#          "\nRun filter_irfinder.R first for these samples.")
#   }
#   id_lists <- lapply(filtered_paths, function(fp) {
#     df <- read.delim(fp, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#     paste0(df$Gene, "/", df$Chr, ":", df$Start, "-", df$End, ":", df$Strand)
#   })
#   all_ids <- unlist(id_lists)
#   tab <- table(all_ids)
#   n <- length(filtered_paths)
#   names(tab)[(tab / n) >= min_frac]
# }
# 
# # ── Output directory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# dir.create("results/ERP171667", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
# 
# 
# # ==============================================================================
# # ERP171667 DATA ANALYSIS
# # ==============================================================================
# 
# paths <- c(
#   file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856082_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856082.txt"),
#   file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856083_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856083.txt"),
#   file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856084_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856084.txt"),
#   file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856085_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856085.txt"),
#   file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856086_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856086.txt"),
#   file.path("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/ERP171667/ERR14856087_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_ERR14856087.txt")
# )
# 
# experiment <- data.frame(
#   condition = factor(
#     c("DMSO","DMSO","DMSO","T025","T025","T025"),
#     levels = c("DMSO","T025")
#   )
# )
# 
# # 1. Initialize with the CORRECT interaction formula
# metaList <- DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder( 
#   filePaths     = paths,
#   designMatrix  = experiment,
#   designFormula = ~ condition + IRFinder + condition:IRFinder
# )
# 
# # Apply the QC file filtering criteria (intron must be present in >= 50% of the 6 samples)
# testable_introns <- get_testable_introns(paths, min_frac = 0.5)
# message("ERP171667: ", length(testable_introns), " / ", nrow(metaList$DESeq2Object),
#         " introns pass filter in >=50% of samples and will be tested")
# # 2. Subset the DESeq2 object down to only the testable introns
# dds_pre_filtered <- metaList$DESeq2Object[rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object) %in% testable_introns, ]
# 
# # ── NEW FILTER: Ensure IR Ratio > 0.1 in at least half (>=3) of the samples ──
# # Calculate actual IR ratios for all testable introns across your samples
# intron_raw_mat <- metaList$IntronDepth
# rownames(intron_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object)
# intron_filtered_mat <- intron_raw_mat[testable_introns, ]
# 
# splice_raw_mat <- metaList$SpliceDepth
# rownames(splice_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object)
# splice_filtered_mat <- splice_raw_mat[testable_introns, ]
# 
# # IR Ratio = Intron / (Intron + Splice)
# all_ir_ratios <- intron_filtered_mat / (intron_filtered_mat + splice_filtered_mat + 0.01)
# 
# # Find rows where at least 3 samples (50% of your 6 samples) have an IR ratio > 0.1
# high_ir_introns <- rownames(all_ir_ratios)[rowSums(all_ir_ratios > 0.1) >= 3]
# 
# # Final clean subset of the dds object
# dds <- dds_pre_filtered[rownames(dds_pre_filtered) %in% high_ir_introns, ]
# 
# message("Filters applied: ", length(high_ir_introns), " / ", length(testable_introns), 
#         " introns also passed the baseline IR ratio > 0.1 threshold.")
# 
# # # 2. Subset the DESeq2 object down to only the testable introns
# # dds <- metaList$DESeq2Object[rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object) %in% testable_introns, ]
# 
# # Extract the specific interaction term that defines Differential Intron Retention
# # 3. Run DESeq2 with the interaction design
# dds <- DESeq(dds)
# 
# # Print the exact names to your log so you can see them
# print("Available coefficients for ERP171667:")
# print(resultsNames(dds))
# 
# # Extract the interaction term
# res <- results(dds, name = "conditionT025.IRFinderIR")
# # res <- results(dds, name = "condition_T025_vs_DMSO.IRFinder.IR")
# 
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res),
#           file = "results/ERP171667/T025_vs_DMSO_1uM_24h.csv")
# 
# message("T-025 1uM 24h vs DMSO (Differential IR): ",
#         sum(res$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
#         " significant introns")
# 
# 
# # ==============================================================================
# # GENERATE CUSTOM INTRON RETENTION PCA (FIXED)
# # ==============================================================================
# message("Generating Intron Retention PCA plot...")
# 
# # 1. Extract raw matrices and explicitly attach row names from the DESeq2 object
# intron_raw <- metaList$IntronDepth
# rownames(intron_raw) <- rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object)
# intron_counts <- intron_raw[testable_introns, ]
# 
# splice_raw <- metaList$SpliceDepth
# rownames(splice_raw) <- rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object)
# splice_counts <- splice_raw[testable_introns, ]
# 
# # 2. Calculate IR Ratio: Intron / (Intron + Splice) 
# # Add a small pseudocount (0.01) to avoid dividing by zero or taking log of zero
# ir_ratio <- intron_counts / (intron_counts + splice_counts + 0.01)
# 
# # 3. Log-transform the ratios for PCA stability
# log_ir <- log2(ir_ratio + 0.01)
# 
# # 4. Remove any rows with zero variance across all samples (will crash PCA)
# log_ir_filtered <- log_ir[apply(log_ir, 1, var) > 0, ]
# 
# # 5. Compute PCA (transpose because prcomp expects samples in rows)
# pca_output <- prcomp(t(log_ir_filtered), scale. = TRUE)
# 
# # Calculate percent variance explained by PC1 and PC2
# percentVar <- round(100 * (pca_output$sdev^2 / sum(pca_output$sdev^2)))
# 
# # 6. Build a dataframe for plotting
# pca_data <- data.frame(
#   PC1 = pca_output$x[, 1],
#   PC2 = pca_output$x[, 2],
#   Condition = experiment$condition,
#   Sample = paste0("Sample_", 1:nrow(experiment))
# )
# 
# # 7. Save the PCA plot using base R graphics
# png("results/ERP171667/IR_Ratio_PCA.png", width = 800, height = 600, res = 120)
# plot(pca_data$PC1, pca_data$PC2, 
#      col = as.numeric(pca_data$Condition) + 1, 
#      pch = 19, cex = 2,
#      xlab = paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance"),
#      ylab = paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance"),
#      main = "PCA of Intron Retention Ratios (ERP171667)")
# grid(lty = "dotted", col = "gray")
# text(pca_data$PC1, pca_data$PC2, labels = pca_data$Sample, pos = 3, cex = 0.8)
# legend("topright", legend = levels(pca_data$Condition), col = 2:3, pch = 19)
# dev.off()
# 
# message("Done! PCA saved to results/ERP171667/IR_Ratio_PCA.png")


# metaList <- DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder( 
#   filePaths     = paths,
#   designMatrix  = experiment,
#   designFormula = ~ condition
# )
# 
# # Apply the QC file filtering criteria (intron must be present in >= 50% of the 6 samples)
# testable_introns <- get_testable_introns(paths, min_frac = 0.5)
# message("ERP171667: ", length(testable_introns), " / ", nrow(metaList$DESeq2Object),
#         " introns pass filter in >=50% of samples and will be tested")
# 
# # Subset the DESeq2 object down to only the testable introns before running DESeq()
# dds <- metaList$DESeq2Object[rownames(metaList$DESeq2Object) %in% testable_introns, ]
# dds <- DESeq(dds)
# 
# res <- results(dds, contrast = c("condition", "T025", "DMSO"))
# 
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res),
#           file = "results/ERP171667/T025_vs_DMSO_1uM_24h.csv")
# 
# message("T-025 1uM 24h vs DMSO: ",
#         sum(res$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
#         " significant introns")
# message("Done!")

Zhu et al. 2018
setwd("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/")
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install(c("SummarizedExperiment", "DESeq2"), ask = FALSE)
library(DESeq2)

# ── Custom IRFinder DESeq2 function (Kept for Raw File Parsing) ─────────────
DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder = function(filePaths, designMatrix, designFormula){
  res=c()
  libsz=c()
  spl=c()
  irtest=read.table(filePaths[1])
  if (irtest[1,1]=="Chr"){irtest=irtest[-1,]}
  irnames=unname(apply(as.matrix(irtest),1,FUN=function(x){return(paste0(x[4],"/",x[1],":",x[2],"-",x[3],":",x[6]))}))
  n=1
  for (i in filePaths){
    print(paste0("processing file ",n," at ",i))
    irtab=read.table(i)
    if (irtab[1,1]=="Chr"){irtab=irtab[-1,]}
    tmp1=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,9]))
    tmp2=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,19]))
    tmp3=tmp1+tmp2
    tmp4=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,17]))
    tmp5=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,18]))
    tmp6=pmax(tmp4,tmp5, na.rm=T)
    res=cbind(res,tmp1)
    libsz=cbind(libsz,tmp2)
    spl=cbind(spl,tmp6)
    n=n+1
  }
  res.rd=round(res)
  libsz.rd=round(libsz)
  spl.rd=round(spl)
  colnames(res.rd)=paste("intronDepth",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
  rownames(res.rd)=irnames
  colnames(libsz.rd)=paste("totalSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
  rownames(libsz.rd)=irnames
  colnames(spl.rd)=paste("maxSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
  rownames(spl.rd)=irnames
  ir=c(rep("IR",dim(designMatrix)[1]),rep("Splice",dim(designMatrix)[1]))
  
  group=rbind(designMatrix,designMatrix)
  group$IRFinder=ir
  group$IRFinder=factor(group$IRFinder,levels=c("Splice","IR"))
  
  counts.IRFinder=cbind(res.rd,spl.rd)
  dd = DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts.IRFinder, colData = group, design = designFormula)
  sizeFactors(dd)=rep(1,dim(group)[1])
  rownames(dd)=irnames
  final=list(dd,res,libsz,spl)
  names(final)=c("DESeq2Object","IntronDepth","SpliceDepth","MaxSplice")
  return(final)
}

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Maps raw path back to the pre-filtered file path to discover the high-quality whitelist
filtered_path_for <- function(raw_path) {
  dir <- dirname(raw_path)
  sample <- sub("_irfinder$", "", basename(dir))
  file.path(dir, paste0("filtered_ir_nondir_", sample, ".txt"))
}

get_testable_introns <- function(raw_paths, min_frac = 0.5) {
  filtered_paths <- vapply(raw_paths, filtered_path_for, character(1))
  missing <- filtered_paths[!file.exists(filtered_paths)]
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop("Missing filtered file(s):\n  ", paste(missing, collapse = "\n  "),
         "\nRun filter_irfinder.R first for these samples.")
  }
  id_lists <- lapply(filtered_paths, function(fp) {
    df <- read.delim(fp, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    paste0(df$Gene, "/", df$Chr, ":", df$Start, "-", df$End, ":", df$Strand)
  })
  all_ids <- unlist(id_lists)
  tab <- table(all_ids)
  n <- length(filtered_paths)
  names(tab)[(tab / n) >= min_frac]
}

dir.create("results/SRP141379", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ============================================================
# POOLED SAMPLE & METADATA CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================
# NOTE: point these directly to the raw IRFinder outputs so the matrix loader functions correctly!
paths_all <- c(
  # 6-Hour Timepoints
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204967_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204969_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204971_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204972_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204973_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204977_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204978_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204979_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  # 24-Hour Timepoints
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204968_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204970_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204974_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204975_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204976_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204980_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204981_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
  "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204982_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"
)

experiment_all <- data.frame(
  condition = c(rep("DMSO", 2), rep("CC671_3uM", 3), rep("CC671_10uM", 3), 
                rep("DMSO", 2), rep("CC671_3uM", 3), rep("CC671_10uM", 3)),
  timepoint = c(rep("6hr", 8), rep("24hr", 8))
)

# Load data using raw file layouts
metaList_all <- DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder(
  filePaths     = paths_all,
  designMatrix  = experiment_all,
  designFormula = ~ 1
)

dds_all_raw <- metaList_all$DESeq2Object

colData(dds_all_raw)$final_group <- factor(
  paste(colData(dds_all_raw)$condition, colData(dds_all_raw)$timepoint, colData(dds_all_raw)$IRFinder, sep = "_")
)
design(dds_all_raw) <- ~ 0 + final_group

# ============================================================
# TWO-STAGE FILTERING STEP (FIXED)
# ============================================================
# Stage 1: Get high quality whitelisted introns from filtered files
testable_all <- get_testable_introns(paths_all, min_frac = 0.5)

# Map row names back to matrices to prevent bounds errors
intron_raw_mat <- metaList_all$IntronDepth
rownames(intron_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList_all$DESeq2Object)
intron_filtered_mat <- intron_raw_mat[testable_all, ]

splice_raw_mat <- metaList_all$SpliceDepth
rownames(splice_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList_all$DESeq2Object)
splice_filtered_mat <- splice_raw_mat[testable_all, ]

# Stage 2: Calculate actual IR values and filter for IR > 0.1
all_ir_ratios <- intron_filtered_mat / (intron_filtered_mat + splice_filtered_mat + 0.01)

# Retain introns with an active IR ratio > 0.1 in >= 2 samples
high_ir_introns_all <- rownames(all_ir_ratios)[rowSums(all_ir_ratios > 0.1) >= 2]

message("Combined Analysis: ", length(testable_all), " introns pass quality screening.")
message("Value Filtering: ", length(high_ir_introns_all), " / ", length(testable_all), 
        " introns also pass baseline expression threshold (IR ratio > 0.1 in >=2 samples).")

# Subset and process final clean dataset
dds_all <- dds_all_raw[rownames(dds_all_raw) %in% high_ir_introns_all, ]
dds_all <- DESeq(dds_all)

levels_available <- resultsNames(dds_all)
print("Available coefficients for custom contrasts:")
print(levels_available)

# ============================================================
# EXTRACTING RESULTS & CALCULATING CONTRASTS
# ============================================================
make_ir_contrast <- function(all_names, drug, tp) {
  vec <- rep(0, length(all_names))
  vec[grep(paste0("final_group", drug, "_", tp, "_IR"),      all_names)] <- 1
  vec[grep(paste0("final_group", drug, "_", tp, "_Splice"),  all_names)] <- -1
  vec[grep(paste0("final_groupDMSO_", tp, "_IR"),            all_names)] <- -1
  vec[grep(paste0("final_groupDMSO_", tp, "_Splice"),        all_names)] <- 1
  return(vec)
}

contrast_6hr_3uM  <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_3uM", "6hr")
contrast_6hr_10uM <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_10uM", "6hr")
res_6hr_3uM  <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_6hr_3uM)
res_6hr_10uM <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_6hr_10uM)

contrast_24hr_3uM  <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_3uM", "24hr")
contrast_24hr_10uM <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_10uM", "24hr")
res_24hr_3uM  <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_24hr_3uM)
res_24hr_10uM <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_24hr_10uM)

write.csv(as.data.frame(res_6hr_3uM),   file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_6hr.csv")
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_6hr_10uM),  file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_6hr.csv")
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_24hr_3uM),  file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_24hr.csv")
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_24hr_10uM), file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_24hr.csv")

message("--- Summary of Significant Intron Retention Events (padj < 0.05) ---")
message("6hr  - CC671 3uM vs DMSO:  ", sum(res_6hr_3uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
message("6hr  - CC671 10uM vs DMSO: ", sum(res_6hr_10uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
message("24hr - CC671 3uM vs DMSO:  ", sum(res_24hr_3uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
message("24hr - CC671 10uM vs DMSO: ", sum(res_24hr_10uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))

# ============================================================
# GENERATE CUSTOM MULTI-CONDITION INTRON RETENTION PCA
# ============================================================
message("Generating Intron Retention multi-factor PCA plot...")

intron_counts <- intron_raw_mat[high_ir_introns_all, ]
splice_counts <- splice_raw_mat[high_ir_introns_all, ]

ir_ratio <- intron_counts / (intron_counts + splice_counts + 0.01)
log_ir <- log2(ir_ratio + 0.01)
log_ir_filtered <- log_ir[apply(log_ir, 1, var) > 0, ]

pca_output <- prcomp(t(log_ir_filtered), scale. = TRUE)
percentVar <- round(100 * (pca_output$sdev^2 / sum(pca_output$sdev^2)))
pca_data <- data.frame(PC1 = pca_output$x[, 1],
                       PC2 = pca_output$x[, 2],
                       Condition = experiment_all$condition,
                       Timepoint = experiment_all$timepoint)

pca_data$ColorCode <- as.numeric(factor(pca_data$Condition, levels = c("DMSO", "CC671_3uM", "CC671_10uM"))) + 1
pca_data$ShapeCode <- ifelse(pca_data$Timepoint == "6hr", 16, 17)

png("results/SRP141379/IR_MultiFactor_PCA.png", width = 900, height = 700, res = 130)

plot(pca_data$PC1, pca_data$PC2,col = pca_data$ColorCode,pch = pca_data$ShapeCode,cex = 2,xlab = paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance"),ylab = paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance"),main = "PCA of Intron Retention Ratios (SRP141379)")

grid(lty = "dotted", col = "gray")

legend("topleft", legend = c("DMSO", "CC671 3uM", "CC671 10uM"), col = c(2, 3, 4), pch = 16, title = "Treatment")
legend("bottomleft", legend = c("6 Hours", "24 Hours"), col = "black", pch = c(16, 17), title = "Timepoint")
dev.off()
message("Done! Multi-factor PCA saved to results/SRP141379/IR_MultiFactor_PCA.png")
# setwd("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/")
# if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install(c("SummarizedExperiment", "DESeq2"), ask = FALSE)
# library(DESeq2)
# 
# # ── Custom IRFinder DESeq2 function ─────────────────────────
# DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder = function(filePaths, designMatrix, designFormula){
#   res=c()
#   libsz=c()
#   spl=c()
#   irtest=read.table(filePaths[1])
#   if (irtest[1,1]=="Chr"){irtest=irtest[-1,]}
#   irnames=unname(apply(as.matrix(irtest),1,FUN=function(x){return(paste0(x[4],"/",x[1],":",x[2],"-",x[3],":",x[6]))}))
#   n=1
#   for (i in filePaths){
#     print(paste0("processing file ",n," at ",i))
#     irtab=read.table(i)
#     if (irtab[1,1]=="Chr"){irtab=irtab[-1,]}
#     tmp1=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,9]))
#     tmp2=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,19]))
#     tmp3=tmp1+tmp2
#     tmp4=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,17]))
#     tmp5=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,18]))
#     tmp6=pmax(tmp4,tmp5, na.rm=T)
#     res=cbind(res,tmp1)
#     libsz=cbind(libsz,tmp2)
#     spl=cbind(spl,tmp6)
#     n=n+1
#   }
#   res.rd=round(res)
#   libsz.rd=round(libsz)
#   spl.rd=round(spl)
#   colnames(res.rd)=paste("intronDepth",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
#   rownames(res.rd)=irnames
#   colnames(libsz.rd)=paste("totalSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
#   rownames(libsz.rd)=irnames
#   colnames(spl.rd)=paste("maxSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
#   rownames(spl.rd)=irnames
#   ir=c(rep("IR",dim(designMatrix)[1]),rep("Splice",dim(designMatrix)[1]))
#   
#   # Duplicate the metadata rows for the IR vs Splice counts
#   group=rbind(designMatrix,designMatrix)
#   group$IRFinder=ir
#   group$IRFinder=factor(group$IRFinder,levels=c("Splice","IR"))
#   
#   counts.IRFinder=cbind(res.rd,spl.rd)
#   dd = DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts.IRFinder, colData = group, design = designFormula)
#   sizeFactors(dd)=rep(1,dim(group)[1])
#   rownames(dd)=irnames
#   final=list(dd,res,libsz,spl)
#   names(final)=c("DESeq2Object","IntronDepth","SpliceDepth","MaxSplice")
#   return(final)
# }
# 
# # ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# filtered_path_for <- function(raw_path) {
#   dir <- dirname(raw_path)
#   sample <- sub("_irfinder$", "", basename(dir))
#   file.path(dir, paste0("filtered_ir_nondir_", sample, ".txt"))
# }
# 
# get_testable_introns <- function(raw_paths, min_frac = 0.5) {
#   filtered_paths <- vapply(raw_paths, filtered_path_for, character(1))
#   missing <- filtered_paths[!file.exists(filtered_paths)]
#   if (length(missing) > 0) {
#     stop("Missing filtered file(s):\n  ", paste(missing, collapse = "\n  "),
#          "\nRun filter_irfinder.R first for these samples.")
#   }
#   id_lists <- lapply(filtered_paths, function(fp) {
#     df <- read.delim(fp, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#     paste0(df$Gene, "/", df$Chr, ":", df$Start, "-", df$End, ":", df$Strand)
#   })
#   all_ids <- unlist(id_lists)
#   tab <- table(all_ids)
#   n <- length(filtered_paths)
#   names(tab)[(tab / n) >= min_frac]
# }
# 
# dir.create("results/SRP141379", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
# 
# # ============================================================
# # POOLED SAMPLE & METADATA CONFIGURATION
# # ============================================================
# 
# paths_all <- c(
#   # 6-Hour Timepoints
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204967_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204967.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204969_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204969.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204971_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204971.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204972_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204972.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204973_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204973.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204977_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204977.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204978_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204978.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204979_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204979.txt",
#   # 24-Hour Timepoints
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204968_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204968.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204970_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204970.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204974_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204974.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204975_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204975.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204976_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204976.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204980_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204980.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204981_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204981.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204982_irfinder/filtered_ir_nondir_SRS3204982.txt"
# )
# 
# # 1. Build base experimental metadata structure
# experiment_all <- data.frame(
#   condition = c(rep("DMSO", 2), rep("CC671_3uM", 3), rep("CC671_10uM", 3), 
#                 rep("DMSO", 2), rep("CC671_3uM", 3), rep("CC671_10uM", 3)),
#   timepoint = c(rep("6hr", 8), rep("24hr", 8))
# )
# 
# # 2. Build the dataset using a plain intercept-only formula (~1) to bypass early validation
# metaList_all <- DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder(
#   filePaths     = paths_all,
#   designMatrix  = experiment_all,
#   designFormula = ~ 1
# )
# 
# # 3. Safely extract object, append final_group, and re-apply our target design matrix
# dds_all_raw <- metaList_all$DESeq2Object
# 
# colData(dds_all_raw)$final_group <- factor(
#   paste(colData(dds_all_raw)$condition, colData(dds_all_raw)$timepoint, colData(dds_all_raw)$IRFinder, sep = "_")
# )
# 
# # Now we safely transition the model over to the no-intercept layout
# design(dds_all_raw) <- ~ 0 + final_group
# 
# # 4. Global QC filter across all 16 files + Baseline value filter (IR > 0.1)
# testable_all <- get_testable_introns(paths_all, min_frac = 0.5)
# 
# # Extract raw matrices and assign valid row names from DESeq2Object
# intron_raw_mat <- metaList_all$IntronDepth
# rownames(intron_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList_all$DESeq2Object)
# intron_filtered_mat <- intron_raw_mat[testable_all, ]
# 
# splice_raw_mat <- metaList_all$SpliceDepth
# rownames(splice_raw_mat) <- rownames(metaList_all$DESeq2Object)
# splice_filtered_mat <- splice_raw_mat[testable_all, ]
# 
# # Calculate baseline IR Ratio matrix
# all_ir_ratios <- intron_filtered_mat / (intron_filtered_mat + splice_filtered_mat + 0.01)
# 
# # Keep introns where at least 2 samples (matching minimum replicate sizes) have an IR ratio > 0.1
# high_ir_introns_all <- rownames(all_ir_ratios)[rowSums(all_ir_ratios > 0.1) >= 2]
# 
# message("Combined Analysis: ", length(testable_all), " introns pass min_frac filter.")
# message("Value Filtering: ", length(high_ir_introns_all), " / ", length(testable_all), 
#         " introns also have an IR ratio > 0.1 in >=2 samples and will be tested.")
# 
# # Subset down to final filtered intron whitelist
# dds_all <- dds_all_raw[rownames(dds_all_raw) %in% high_ir_introns_all, ]
# dds_all <- DESeq(dds_all)
# 
# # Print explicit names available to make sure contrasts match up perfectly
# levels_available <- resultsNames(dds_all)
# print("Available coefficients for custom contrasts:")
# print(levels_available)
# 
# # ============================================================
# # EXTRACTING RESULTS VIA EXPLICIT NUMERIC VECTORS
# # ============================================================
# make_ir_contrast <- function(all_names, drug, tp) {
#   vec <- rep(0, length(all_names))
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_group", drug, "_", tp, "_IR"),      all_names)] <- 1
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_group", drug, "_", tp, "_Splice"),  all_names)] <- -1
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_groupDMSO_", tp, "_IR"),            all_names)] <- -1
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_groupDMSO_", tp, "_Splice"),        all_names)] <- 1
#   return(vec)
# }
# 
# # ── 6-Hour Timepoint Contrasts ──────────────────────────────
# contrast_6hr_3uM  <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_3uM", "6hr")
# contrast_6hr_10uM <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_10uM", "6hr")
# 
# res_6hr_3uM  <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_6hr_3uM)
# res_6hr_10uM <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_6hr_10uM)
# 
# # ── 24-Hour Timepoint Contrasts ─────────────────────────────
# contrast_24hr_3uM  <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_3uM", "24hr")
# contrast_24hr_10uM <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_10uM", "24hr")
# 
# res_24hr_3uM  <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_24hr_3uM)
# res_24hr_10uM <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_24hr_10uM)
# 
# # ── Save Outputs ────────────────────────────────────────────
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_6hr_3uM),   file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_6hr.csv")
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_6hr_10uM),  file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_6hr.csv")
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_24hr_3uM),  file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_24hr.csv")
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_24hr_10uM), file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_24hr.csv")
# 
# # Print Summary Run Metrics
# message("--- Summary of Significant Intron Retention Events (padj < 0.05) ---")
# message("6hr  - CC671 3uM vs DMSO:  ", sum(res_6hr_3uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
# message("6hr  - CC671 10uM vs DMSO: ", sum(res_6hr_10uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
# message("24hr - CC671 3uM vs DMSO:  ", sum(res_24hr_3uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
# message("24hr - CC671 10uM vs DMSO: ", sum(res_24hr_10uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
# 
# 
# # ============================================================
# # GENERATE CUSTOM MULTI-CONDITION INTRON RETENTION PCA
# # ============================================================
# message("Generating Intron Retention multi-factor PCA plot...")
# 
# # 1. Extract raw matrices and explicitly attach row names from the DESeq2 object
# intron_raw <- metaList_all$IntronDepth
# rownames(intron_raw) <- rownames(metaList_all$DESeq2Object)
# intron_counts <- intron_raw[testable_all, ]
# 
# splice_raw <- metaList_all$SpliceDepth
# rownames(splice_raw) <- rownames(metaList_all$DESeq2Object)
# splice_counts <- splice_raw[testable_all, ]
# 
# # 2. Calculate IR Ratio: Intron / (Intron + Splice) 
# # Add small pseudocount (0.01) to protect against mathematical zeroes
# ir_ratio <- intron_counts / (intron_counts + splice_counts + 0.01)
# 
# # 3. Log-transform and filter invariant rows
# log_ir <- log2(ir_ratio + 0.01)
# log_ir_filtered <- log_ir[apply(log_ir, 1, var) > 0, ]
# 
# # 4. Compute PCA (transpose because columns are samples)
# pca_output <- prcomp(t(log_ir_filtered), scale. = TRUE)
# percentVar <- round(100 * (pca_output$sdev^2 / sum(pca_output$sdev^2)))
# 
# # 5. Build PCA dataframe mapping back to your 16 individual samples
# pca_data <- data.frame(
#   PC1       = pca_output$x[, 1],
#   PC2       = pca_output$x[, 2],
#   Condition = experiment_all$condition,
#   Timepoint = experiment_all$timepoint,
#   Group     = paste(experiment_all$condition, experiment_all$timepoint, sep = "_")
# )
# 
# # Assign distinct plotting visual features (colors for drug, shapes for time)
# pca_data$ColorCode <- as.numeric(factor(pca_data$Condition, levels = c("DMSO", "CC671_3uM", "CC671_10uM"))) + 1
# pca_data$ShapeCode <- ifelse(pca_data$Timepoint == "6hr", 16, 17) # Circle=6hr, Triangle=24hr
# 
# # 6. Save Plot
# png("results/SRP141379/IR_MultiFactor_PCA.png", width = 900, height = 700, res = 130)
# plot(pca_data$PC1, pca_data$PC2, 
#      col = pca_data$ColorCode, 
#      pch = pca_data$ShapeCode, 
#      cex = 2,
#      xlab = paste0("PC1: ", percentVar[1], "% variance"),
#      ylab = paste0("PC2: ", percentVar[2], "% variance"),
#      main = "PCA of Intron Retention Ratios (SRP141379)")
# 
# grid(lty = "dotted", col = "gray")
# 
# # Add a distinct legend configuration split by Factor
# legend("topleft", 
#        legend = c("DMSO", "CC671 3uM", "CC671 10uM"), 
#        col = c(2, 3, 4), 
#        pch = 16, 
#        title = "Treatment")
# 
# legend("bottomleft", 
#        legend = c("6 Hours", "24 Hours"), 
#        col = "black", 
#        pch = c(16, 17), 
#        title = "Timepoint")
# 
# dev.off()
# 
# message("Done! Multi-factor PCA saved to results/SRP141379/IR_MultiFactor_PCA.png")


# setwd("/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/")
# if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
# BiocManager::install(c("SummarizedExperiment", "DESeq2"), ask = FALSE)
# library(DESeq2)
# 
# # ── Custom IRFinder DESeq2 function ─────────────────────────
# DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder = function(filePaths, designMatrix, designFormula){
#   res=c()
#   libsz=c()
#   spl=c()
#   irtest=read.table(filePaths[1])
#   if (irtest[1,1]=="Chr"){irtest=irtest[-1,]}
#   irnames=unname(apply(as.matrix(irtest),1,FUN=function(x){return(paste0(x[4],"/",x[1],":",x[2],"-",x[3],":",x[6]))}))
#   n=1
#   for (i in filePaths){
#     print(paste0("processing file ",n," at ",i))
#     irtab=read.table(i)
#     if (irtab[1,1]=="Chr"){irtab=irtab[-1,]}
#     tmp1=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,9]))
#     tmp2=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,19]))
#     tmp3=tmp1+tmp2
#     tmp4=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,17]))
#     tmp5=as.numeric(as.vector(irtab[,18]))
#     tmp6=pmax(tmp4,tmp5, na.rm=T)
#     res=cbind(res,tmp1)
#     libsz=cbind(libsz,tmp2)
#     spl=cbind(spl,tmp6)
#     n=n+1
#   }
#   res.rd=round(res)
#   libsz.rd=round(libsz)
#   spl.rd=round(spl)
#   colnames(res.rd)=paste("intronDepth",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
#   rownames(res.rd)=irnames
#   colnames(libsz.rd)=paste("totalSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
#   rownames(libsz.rd)=irnames
#   colnames(spl.rd)=paste("maxSplice",as.vector(designMatrix[,1]),sep=".")
#   rownames(spl.rd)=irnames
#   ir=c(rep("IR",dim(designMatrix)[1]),rep("Splice",dim(designMatrix)[1]))
#   
#   # Duplicate the metadata rows for the IR vs Splice counts
#   group=rbind(designMatrix,designMatrix)
#   group$IRFinder=ir
#   group$IRFinder=factor(group$IRFinder,levels=c("Splice","IR"))
#   
#   counts.IRFinder=cbind(res.rd,spl.rd)
#   dd = DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts.IRFinder, colData = group, design = designFormula)
#   sizeFactors(dd)=rep(1,dim(group)[1])
#   rownames(dd)=irnames
#   final=list(dd,res,libsz,spl)
#   names(final)=c("DESeq2Object","IntronDepth","SpliceDepth","MaxSplice")
#   return(final)
# }
# 
# # ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# filtered_path_for <- function(raw_path) {
#   dir <- dirname(raw_path)
#   sample <- sub("_irfinder$", "", basename(dir))
#   file.path(dir, paste0("filtered_ir_nondir_", sample, ".txt"))
# }
# 
# get_testable_introns <- function(raw_paths, min_frac = 0.5) {
#   filtered_paths <- vapply(raw_paths, filtered_path_for, character(1))
#   missing <- filtered_paths[!file.exists(filtered_paths)]
#   if (length(missing) > 0) {
#     stop("Missing filtered file(s):\n  ", paste(missing, collapse = "\n  "),
#          "\nRun filter_irfinder.R first for these samples.")
#   }
#   id_lists <- lapply(filtered_paths, function(fp) {
#     df <- read.delim(fp, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#     paste0(df$Gene, "/", df$Chr, ":", df$Start, "-", df$End, ":", df$Strand)
#   })
#   all_ids <- unlist(id_lists)
#   tab <- table(all_ids)
#   n <- length(filtered_paths)
#   names(tab)[(tab / n) >= min_frac]
# }
# 
# dir.create("results/SRP141379", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
# 
# # ============================================================
# # POOLED SAMPLE & METADATA CONFIGURATION (FIXED)
# # ============================================================
# 
# paths_all <- c(
#   # 6-Hour Timepoints
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204967_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204969_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204971_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204972_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204973_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204977_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204978_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204979_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   # 24-Hour Timepoints
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204968_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204970_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204974_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204975_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204976_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204980_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204981_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt",
#   "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/raw_data/IR_quantification/SRP141379/SRS3204982_irfinder/IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt"
# )
# 
# # 1. Build base experimental metadata structure
# experiment_all <- data.frame(
#   condition = c(rep("DMSO", 2), rep("CC671_3uM", 3), rep("CC671_10uM", 3), 
#                 rep("DMSO", 2), rep("CC671_3uM", 3), rep("CC671_10uM", 3)),
#   timepoint = c(rep("6hr", 8), rep("24hr", 8))
# )
# 
# # 2. Build the dataset using a plain intercept-only formula (~1) to bypass early validation
# metaList_all <- DESeqDataSetFromIRFinder(
#   filePaths     = paths_all,
#   designMatrix  = experiment_all,
#   designFormula = ~ 1
# )
# 
# # 3. Safely extract object, append final_group, and re-apply our target design matrix
# dds_all_raw <- metaList_all$DESeq2Object
# 
# colData(dds_all_raw)$final_group <- factor(
#   paste(colData(dds_all_raw)$condition, colData(dds_all_raw)$timepoint, colData(dds_all_raw)$IRFinder, sep = "_")
# )
# 
# # Now we safely transition the model over to the no-intercept layout
# design(dds_all_raw) <- ~ 0 + final_group
# 
# # 4. Global QC filter across all 16 files
# testable_all <- get_testable_introns(paths_all, min_frac = 0.5)
# message("Combined Analysis: ", length(testable_all), " / ", nrow(dds_all_raw),
#         " introns pass filter in >=50% of all samples and will be tested.")
# 
# dds_all <- dds_all_raw[rownames(dds_all_raw) %in% testable_all, ]
# dds_all <- DESeq(dds_all)
# 
# # Print explicit names available to make sure contrasts match up perfectly
# levels_available <- resultsNames(dds_all)
# print("Available coefficients for custom contrasts:")
# print(levels_available)
# 
# # ============================================================
# # EXTRACTING RESULTS VIA EXPLICIT NUMERIC VECTORS
# # ============================================================
# make_ir_contrast <- function(all_names, drug, tp) {
#   vec <- rep(0, length(all_names))
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_group", drug, "_", tp, "_IR"),      all_names)] <- 1
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_group", drug, "_", tp, "_Splice"),  all_names)] <- -1
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_groupDMSO_", tp, "_IR"),            all_names)] <- -1
#   vec[grep(paste0("final_groupDMSO_", tp, "_Splice"),        all_names)] <- 1
#   return(vec)
# }
# 
# # ── 6-Hour Timepoint Contrasts ──────────────────────────────
# contrast_6hr_3uM  <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_3uM", "6hr")
# contrast_6hr_10uM <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_10uM", "6hr")
# 
# res_6hr_3uM  <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_6hr_3uM)
# res_6hr_10uM <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_6hr_10uM)
# 
# # ── 24-Hour Timepoint Contrasts ─────────────────────────────
# contrast_24hr_3uM  <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_3uM", "24hr")
# contrast_24hr_10uM <- make_ir_contrast(levels_available, "CC671_10uM", "24hr")
# 
# res_24hr_3uM  <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_24hr_3uM)
# res_24hr_10uM <- results(dds_all, contrast = contrast_24hr_10uM)
# 
# # Save Outputs
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_6hr_3uM),   file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_6hr_pooled.csv")
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_6hr_10uM),  file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_6hr_pooled.csv")
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_24hr_3uM),  file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_24hr_pooled.csv")
# write.csv(as.data.frame(res_24hr_10uM), file = "results/SRP141379/CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_24hr_pooled.csv")
# 
# # Print Summary Statistics
# message("6hr — CC671 3uM  vs DMSO: ", sum(res_6hr_3uM$padj  < 0.05, na.rm=TRUE), " significant introns")
# message("6hr — CC671 10uM vs DMSO: ", sum(res_6hr_10uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm=TRUE), " significant introns")
# message("24hr — CC671 3uM  vs DMSO: ", sum(res_24hr_3uM$padj  < 0.05, na.rm=TRUE), " significant introns")
# message("24hr — CC671 10uM vs DMSO: ", sum(res_24hr_10uM$padj < 0.05, na.rm=TRUE), " significant introns")
# 
# message("Done!")

Need to consider how I structure this
Pooling means you've fit one model containing all 16 samples, rather than fitting separate models for:

6 hr only
24 hr only

or

3 µM only
10 µM only

But even if you fit separate models, each model would still return one row per retained intron for that model.

Should you analyze each time point separately?
That depends on your biological question.

Option 1 (your current approach)

You pool all samples and specify contrasts.

Advantages:

More samples contribute to estimating dispersion.
Better variance estimates.
Standard DESeq2 practice.

Disadvantage:
Your filtering is based on all samples together.

Option 2: Separate analyses

For example:
6 hr only
24 hr only

Then compare

CC671 3 µM vs DMSO
CC671 10 µM vs DMSO

within each time point.

Advantages:

Filters are specific to each time point.
You may detect introns expressed only at one time point.

Disadvantages:

Fewer samples.
Less stable dispersion estimates.
What I would change

I actually think the main issue isn't the pooled DESeq analysis.

It's this filter:

testable_all <- get_testable_introns(paths_all, min_frac = 0.5)

and

high_ir_introns_all <- ...

These are computed using all 16 samples together.

Imagine an intron that is only retained at 24 hr.

If it fails your global filter because it isn't present often enough across all 16 samples, it never gets tested—even though it might be biologically interesting at 24 hr.

A better strategy is often:

build the whitelist separately for 6 hr and 24 hr,
or even separately for each comparison,
then run DESeq on those subsets.
Should you make secondary CSVs?

Yes—I think that's a good idea.

You could keep your current pooled analysis as the primary analysis, because it uses all samples to estimate dispersions.

Then create secondary analyses such as:

6 hr only
DMSO vs 3 µM
DMSO vs 10 µM
24 hr only
DMSO vs 3 µM
DMSO vs 10 µM

These won't necessarily have different numbers of rows because results() still returns every retained intron, but the set of retained introns may differ if you perform the filtering separately for each subset.

One thing I'd also look at is your design. You're encoding treatment, time point, and IR/Splice into a single final_group factor and then constructing custom contrasts. That can work, but it's more complex than a standard factorial DESeq2 design (e.g. ~ timepoint + condition + IRFinder + interactions). If your goal is to compare IR changes between treatments at each time point, it's worth checking whether this custom contrast is testing exactly the biological effect you intend.
After running this script you should get:
results/
└── SRP141379/
    ├── 6hr/
    │   ├── CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_6hr.csv
    │   ├── CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_6hr.csv
    │   └── IR_PCA_6hr.png
    │
    └── 24hr/
        ├── CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO_24hr.csv
        ├── CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO_24hr.csv
        └── IR_PCA_24hr.png

One thing I would check after the first run is the printed output:

xxx introns passed whitelist.
xxx introns passed IR filter.
Significant introns (padj < 0.05): xxx

Compare those numbers against your pooled analysis. The important comparison is not the number of rows in the CSVs (those will always equal the tested introns), but whether:

the number of tested introns changes,
the number of significant introns changes,
and whether new biologically interesting introns appear in the timepoint-specific results.

Also, one correction from the earlier discussion: this version still uses your custom IRFinder-style DESeq2 setup with sizeFactors(dds) = 1, which is appropriate for the IR-versus-splice comparison. I would keep that consistent with your original analysis rather than allowing standard DESeq2 normalization to alter the interpretation.
Summaries
Mean IR ratio per sample across all studies
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ============================================================
# Summarise mean IR ratio per sample across all studies
# ============================================================
set -euo pipefail

OUTFILE="results/IR_summary_all_samples.txt"
# mkdir -p results

# Write header
echo -e "Study\tSample\tN_reliable_introns\tMean_IR_ratio\tMedian_IR_ratio\tN_IR_gt0.1\tN_IR_gt0.2" > "$OUTFILE"

find raw_data/IR_quantification -name "IRFinder-IR-nondir.txt" | sort | while read f; do
  sample=$(echo "$f" | awk -F'/' '{print $(NF-1)}')
  study=$(echo "$f"  | awk -F'/' '{print $(NF-2)}')

  awk -v samp="$sample" -v study="$study" '
    NR==1 && $1=="Chr" { next }
    $21 ~ /LowCover|LowSplicing/ { next }
    $20 == "NA" || $20 == "" { next }
    {
      ir = $20 + 0
      sum += ir
      sumsq += ir^2
      n++
      vals[n] = ir
      if (ir > 0.1) gt01++
      if (ir > 0.2) gt02++
    }
    END {
      if (n == 0) {
        print study "\t" samp "\t0\tNA\tNA\t0\t0"
        exit
      }
      mean = sum / n
      # median via sort
      asort(vals)
      if (n % 2 == 1) median = vals[int(n/2)+1]
      else median = (vals[n/2] + vals[n/2+1]) / 2
      printf "%s\t%s\t%d\t%.4f\t%.4f\t%d\t%d\n", \
        study, samp, n, mean, median, gt01, gt02
    }
  ' "$f"
done >> "$OUTFILE"

echo "Done! Results written to $OUTFILE"
cat "$OUTFILE"

Sequence counts
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# =====================================================================
# 01_run_featureCounts.sh
#
# Finds every "Sorted.bam" under raw_data/IR_quantification/<STUDY>/*_irfinder/
# and runs featureCounts on all of them together to build one gene-level
# count matrix, ready for DESeq2.
#
# Strandedness (-s 2, reverse-stranded) was confirmed empirically:
#   ERP171667 test BAM: -s 2 -> 93.7% assigned (vs 90.0% at -s 0, 7.3% at -s 1)
#   SRP141379 test BAM: -s 2 -> 92.5% assigned (vs 89.2% at -s 0, 5.8% at -s 1)
#   featureCounts also auto-detected "reversely stranded" at -s 2 for both.
#
# Paired-end mode (-p -P) is used because these libraries
# ARE paired-end (confirmed in the test run logs: "Paired-end : yes").
# Running in single-end mode would count individual reads instead of
# fragments and bias the counts -- don't drop -p here.
#
# Requires: subread (featureCounts) on PATH.
#   conda install -c bioconda subread
# =====================================================================

# ---- EDIT THESE -------------------------------------------------------
BASE_DIR="raw_data/IR_quantification"          # top-level folder holding the study dirs
GTF="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/IRFinder-repo/REF/Human-GRCh38-release100/OriginalRef/Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.100.gtf.gz"
OUT_DIR="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/DE_gene_counts_stranded"
THREADS=16
STRANDEDNESS=2    # confirmed reverse-stranded, see notes above

# Only these studies are part of this analysis (SRP112607 is excluded).
STUDIES=("ERP171667" "SRP141379")
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

mkdir -p "${OUT_DIR}"

# 1. Collect all Sorted.bam files, only from the studies listed above
echo "Searching for Sorted.bam files under ${BASE_DIR}/{${STUDIES[*]}} ..."
BAMS=()
for study in "${STUDIES[@]}"; do
    while IFS= read -r -d '' bam; do
        BAMS+=("${bam}")
    done < <(find "${BASE_DIR}/${study}" -type f -name "Sorted.bam" -print0)
done
IFS=$'\n' BAMS=($(sort <<<"${BAMS[*]}")); unset IFS

if [ "${#BAMS[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: No Sorted.bam files found under ${BASE_DIR}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Found ${#BAMS[@]} BAM files:"
printf '  %s\n' "${BAMS[@]}"

# 2. Build a sample-name manifest (so we can later map featureCounts' ugly
#    column headers - the full BAM path - back to clean sample IDs).
#    Sample ID = the *_irfinder directory name, minus the _irfinder suffix.
MANIFEST="${OUT_DIR}/sample_manifest.tsv"
echo -e "sample_id\tstudy\tbam_path" > "${MANIFEST}"
for bam in "${BAMS[@]}"; do
    irdir=$(basename "$(dirname "${bam}")")           # e.g. SAMPLE1_irfinder
    sample_id="${irdir%_irfinder}"                     # strip suffix -> SAMPLE1
    study=$(basename "$(dirname "$(dirname "${bam}")")")
    echo -e "${sample_id}\t${study}\t${bam}" >> "${MANIFEST}"
done
echo "Wrote sample manifest: ${MANIFEST}"

# 3. Run featureCounts (paired-end, reverse-stranded) across all BAMs at once.
#    -p specifies paired-end data.
#    -P tells featureCounts to count fragments (read pairs) instead of individual reads.
#    Running all BAMs in a single multi-threaded call (rather than one at a
#    time with 1 thread, as in the test runs) should be considerably faster
#    than the ~100-240 min/file seen in testing.
echo "Running featureCounts (paired-end, fragment-counting, reverse-stranded) across ${#BAMS[@]} BAMs..."
featureCounts \
    -T "${THREADS}" \
    -p \
    -P \
    -B \
    -s "${STRANDEDNESS}" \
    -a "${GTF}" \
    -o "${OUT_DIR}/gene_counts.txt" \
    "${BAMS[@]}"

echo "Done. Raw featureCounts output: ${OUT_DIR}/gene_counts.txt"
echo "Summary stats:                  ${OUT_DIR}/gene_counts.txt.summary"



Gene expression
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# =====================================================================
# 01_run_featureCounts.sh
#
# Finds every "Sorted.bam" under raw_data/IR_quantification/<STUDY>/*_irfinder/
# and runs featureCounts on all of them together to build one gene-level
# count matrix, ready for DESeq2.
#
# Strandedness (-s 2, reverse-stranded) was confirmed empirically:
#   ERP171667 test BAM: -s 2 -> 93.7% assigned (vs 90.0% at -s 0, 7.3% at -s 1)
#   SRP141379 test BAM: -s 2 -> 92.5% assigned (vs 89.2% at -s 0, 5.8% at -s 1)
#   featureCounts also auto-detected "reversely stranded" at -s 2 for both.
#
# Paired-end mode (-p -P) is used because these libraries
# ARE paired-end (confirmed in the test run logs: "Paired-end : yes").
# Running in single-end mode would count individual reads instead of
# fragments and bias the counts -- don't drop -p here.
#
# Requires: subread (featureCounts) on PATH.
#   conda install -c bioconda subread
# =====================================================================

# ---- EDIT THESE -------------------------------------------------------
BASE_DIR="raw_data/IR_quantification"          # top-level folder holding the study dirs
GTF="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/IRFinder-repo/REF/Human-GRCh38-release100/OriginalRef/Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.100.gtf.gz"
OUT_DIR="/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/DE_gene_counts_stranded"
THREADS=16
STRANDEDNESS=2    # confirmed reverse-stranded, see notes above

# Only these studies are part of this analysis (SRP112607 is excluded).
STUDIES=("ERP171667" "SRP141379")
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

mkdir -p "${OUT_DIR}"

# 1. Collect all Sorted.bam files, only from the studies listed above
echo "Searching for Sorted.bam files under ${BASE_DIR}/{${STUDIES[*]}} ..."
BAMS=()
for study in "${STUDIES[@]}"; do
    while IFS= read -r -d '' bam; do
        BAMS+=("${bam}")
    done < <(find "${BASE_DIR}/${study}" -type f -name "Sorted.bam" -print0)
done
IFS=$'\n' BAMS=($(sort <<<"${BAMS[*]}")); unset IFS

if [ "${#BAMS[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: No Sorted.bam files found under ${BASE_DIR}" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Found ${#BAMS[@]} BAM files:"
printf '  %s\n' "${BAMS[@]}"

# 2. Build a sample-name manifest (so we can later map featureCounts' ugly
#    column headers - the full BAM path - back to clean sample IDs).
#    Sample ID = the *_irfinder directory name, minus the _irfinder suffix.
MANIFEST="${OUT_DIR}/sample_manifest.tsv"
echo -e "sample_id\tstudy\tbam_path" > "${MANIFEST}"
for bam in "${BAMS[@]}"; do
    irdir=$(basename "$(dirname "${bam}")")           # e.g. SAMPLE1_irfinder
    sample_id="${irdir%_irfinder}"                     # strip suffix -> SAMPLE1
    study=$(basename "$(dirname "$(dirname "${bam}")")")
    echo -e "${sample_id}\t${study}\t${bam}" >> "${MANIFEST}"
done
echo "Wrote sample manifest: ${MANIFEST}"

# 3. Run featureCounts (paired-end, reverse-stranded) across all BAMs at once.
#    -p specifies paired-end data.
#    -P tells featureCounts to count fragments (read pairs) instead of individual reads.
#    Running all BAMs in a single multi-threaded call (rather than one at a
#    time with 1 thread, as in the test runs) should be considerably faster
#    than the ~100-240 min/file seen in testing.
echo "Running featureCounts (paired-end, fragment-counting, reverse-stranded) across ${#BAMS[@]} BAMs..."
featureCounts \
    -T "${THREADS}" \
    -p \
    -P \
    -B \
    -s "${STRANDEDNESS}" \
    -a "${GTF}" \
    -o "${OUT_DIR}/gene_counts.txt" \
    "${BAMS[@]}"

echo "Done. Raw featureCounts output: ${OUT_DIR}/gene_counts.txt"
echo "Summary stats:                  ${OUT_DIR}/gene_counts.txt.summary"



DESeq gene expression
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript

# =====================================================================
# 02_deseq2_analysis.R
#
# Reads the featureCounts output produced by 01_run_featureCounts.sh,
# merges it with metadata/sample_metadata.tsv (built from
# metadata_sc5903_mw_de_gene.csv), and runs TWO separate DESeq2 models,
# one per study, using the dose-separated `condition` column:
#
#   1. SRP141379 (CAL51, 6hr + 24hr):
#        condition levels: DMSO (ref), CC671_3uM, CC671_10uM
#        design = ~ timepoint_hr + condition
#        contrasts: CC671_3uM vs DMSO, CC671_10uM vs DMSO
#        (timepoint included as an additive covariate; the CC-671 doses
#         are kept separate rather than pooled into a single Treatment
#         group.)
#
#   2. ERP171667 (MDA-MB-231, 24h):
#        condition levels: DMSO (ref), T025
#        design = ~ condition
#        contrast: T025 vs DMSO
#
# SRP112607 is not present in metadata_sc5903_mw_de_gene.csv and is
# therefore excluded automatically.
#
# Requires: DESeq2
#   install.packages("BiocManager")
#   BiocManager::install("DESeq2")
# =====================================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(DESeq2)
})

# ---- EDIT THESE --------------------------------------------------------
counts_dir    <- "/bioinformatics/moniquew/BrCa-Special-Topics/DE_gene_counts_stranded"  # OUT_DIR from 01_run_featureCounts.sh
counts_file   <- file.path(counts_dir, "gene_counts.txt")
manifest_file <- file.path(counts_dir, "sample_manifest.tsv")
metadata_file <- "sample_metadata.tsv"    # sample_id / study / timepoint_hr / cell_line / therapy / concentration / exp_design / condition

out_dir <- "deseq2_results"
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(out_dir, "SRP141379"), showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(file.path(out_dir, "ERP171667"), showWarnings = FALSE)

## 1. Load featureCounts matrix -------------------------------------------
fc <- read.delim(counts_file, comment.char = "#", check.names = FALSE)
gene_ids  <- fc$Geneid
count_mat <- as.matrix(fc[, 7:ncol(fc)])
rownames(count_mat) <- gene_ids

## 2. Map ugly BAM-path column names -> clean sample IDs ------------------
manifest <- read.delim(manifest_file, check.names = FALSE)
path_to_id <- setNames(manifest$sample_id, manifest$bam_path)

matched <- path_to_id[colnames(count_mat)]
if (any(is.na(matched))) {
  stop(
    "Could not map some featureCounts columns to sample IDs via the manifest.\n",
    "Unmatched columns:\n  ",
    paste(colnames(count_mat)[is.na(matched)], collapse = "\n  ")
  )
}
colnames(count_mat) <- matched

## 3. Load metadata ---------------------------------------------------------
metadata <- read.delim(metadata_file, check.names = FALSE)
rownames(metadata) <- metadata$sample_id
metadata$timepoint_hr <- factor(metadata$timepoint_hr)

missing_in_meta <- setdiff(colnames(count_mat), rownames(metadata))
if (length(missing_in_meta) > 0) {
  message(
    "Note: dropping counted samples with no metadata entry (e.g. SRP112607, not in metadata_sc5903_mw_de_gene.csv):\n  ",
    paste(missing_in_meta, collapse = "\n  ")
  )
}

run_deseq_model <- function(study_name, design_formula, factor_cols, ref_levels,
                             contrasts, out_subdir) {

  message("\n=== ", study_name, " ===")

  sub_meta <- metadata[metadata$study == study_name, , drop = FALSE]
  sub_meta <- sub_meta[rownames(sub_meta) %in% colnames(count_mat), , drop = FALSE]

  missing_counts <- setdiff(rownames(sub_meta), colnames(count_mat))
  if (length(missing_counts) > 0) {
    stop(study_name, ": these samples have metadata but no count data:\n  ",
         paste(missing_counts, collapse = "\n  "))
  }

  sub_counts <- count_mat[, rownames(sub_meta), drop = FALSE]

  for (col in names(factor_cols)) {
    sub_meta[[col]] <- factor(sub_meta[[col]])
    if (!is.null(ref_levels[[col]])) {
      sub_meta[[col]] <- relevel(sub_meta[[col]], ref = ref_levels[[col]])
    }
  }

  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = sub_counts,
    colData   = sub_meta,
    design    = design_formula
  )
  dds <- dds[rowSums(counts(dds)) >= 10, ]
  dds <- DESeq(dds)

  for (contrast_name in names(contrasts)) {
    res <- results(dds, contrast = contrasts[[contrast_name]])
    res_ordered <- res[order(res$padj), ]
    out_file <- file.path(out_dir, out_subdir, paste0(contrast_name, ".csv"))
    write.csv(as.data.frame(res_ordered), out_file)
    message(
      "  ", contrast_name, ": ",
      sum(res$padj < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), " significant genes  -> ", out_file
    )
  }

  norm_counts <- counts(dds, normalized = TRUE)
  write.csv(norm_counts, file.path(out_dir, out_subdir, "normalized_counts.csv"))

  vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)
  pdf(file.path(out_dir, out_subdir, "PCA_plot.pdf"))
  intgroup <- names(factor_cols)
  print(plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = intgroup))
  dev.off()

  dds
}

## 4. SRP141379 -- combined timepoint model, dose-separated condition ------
dds_141379 <- run_deseq_model(
  study_name     = "SRP141379",
  design_formula = ~ timepoint_hr + condition,
  factor_cols    = list(timepoint_hr = NULL, condition = NULL),
  ref_levels     = list(timepoint_hr = "6", condition = "DMSO"),
  contrasts      = list(
    "CC671_3uM_vs_DMSO"  = c("condition", "CC671_3uM",  "DMSO"),
    "CC671_10uM_vs_DMSO" = c("condition", "CC671_10uM", "DMSO")
  ),
  out_subdir = "SRP141379"
)

## 5. ERP171667 -- standalone model, dose-separated condition ---------------
dds_ERP171667 <- run_deseq_model(
  study_name     = "ERP171667",
  design_formula = ~ condition,
  factor_cols    = list(condition = NULL),
  ref_levels     = list(condition = "DMSO"),
  contrasts      = list(
    "T025_vs_DMSO" = c("condition", "T025", "DMSO")
  ),
  out_subdir = "ERP171667"
)

message("\nDone. Results written under: ", out_dir)
